name: Vuelta al Barranco Ancho
name_original: Obón - Torre de las Arcas - Peñas Royas - Montalbán - La Hoz de la Vieja — circular desde Josa
description: This difficult 39.1-mile mountain biking route in Parque Cultural del Río Martín offers diverse landscapes and challenging terrain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Josa features a diverse landscape characterized by significant elevation changes and varied terrain. The region includes routes that traverse river valleys, open plains, and more rugged, hilly areas. Riders can expect paths that wind through natural formations and offer expansive views across the landscape. Best mountain bike trails around Josa The most popular mountain bike trail is Vuelta al Barranco Ancho, a 39.1 miles (62.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 44 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Muniesa - Huesa del Común -, a difficult 38.5 miles (61.9 km) path. This route offers a challenging ride with nearly 3,200 feet of climbing through the regional landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Martín River Route (BTT 01), a 22.3 miles (35.9 km) trail leading through the Martín River valley, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. Mountain biking around Josa is defined by river valleys, open plains, and hilly sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Josa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Josa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Josa?
A: There are 16 mountain bike trails available around Josa. These routes offer a diverse landscape with significant elevation changes and varied terrain, suitable for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails for beginners in Josa?
A: Yes, Josa offers one easy mountain bike trail, providing a gentle introduction to the region's diverse terrain. The area generally features routes ranging from moderate to difficult, so beginners should look for trails specifically marked as easy.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Josa?
A: Mountain biking around Josa is characterized by river valleys, open plains, and rugged, hilly sections. You can expect paths that wind through natural formations and offer expansive views across the landscape, with significant elevation changes throughout the region.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Josa?
A: Absolutely. Josa features 6 difficult mountain bike trails for experienced riders. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006865421/vuelta-al-barranco-ancho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Barranco Ancho&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006847693/muniesa-huesa-del-comun" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muniesa - Huesa del Común -&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial elevation gain and challenging rides through varied terrain, often taking several hours to complete.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Josa?
A: The mountain bike routes in Josa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Josa?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes in Josa. An example is the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996262168/obon-alcaine-y-embalse-de-cueva-foradada-circular-desde-hoz-de-la-vieja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obón, Alcaine y Embalse de Cueva Foradada — circular desde Hoz de la Vieja&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 72 km and includes significant climbing, offering a full loop experience.

Q: What are some scenic mountain bike trails in Josa?
A: Many trails in Josa offer scenic views due to the region's diverse landscape of river valleys, open plains, and hilly sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996649204/martin-river-route-btt-01" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Martín River Route (BTT 01)&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through the picturesque Martín River valley, providing a moderate ride with beautiful surroundings.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Josa?
A: While riding the mountain bike trails around Josa, you can encounter several interesting points. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3618839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Foradada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the charming settlements of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5143126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obón&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5143010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz de la Vieja&lt;/a&gt;. There's also the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4596234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Ana&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into local heritage.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Josa?
A: While the guide highlights routes from moderate to difficult, the presence of an easy trail suggests there are options suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. It's best to check the difficulty rating of individual routes to ensure they align with your family's skill level.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Josa?
A: Given the region's diverse terrain and potential for significant elevation changes, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Josa, with milder temperatures. Summer can be warm, and winter might bring colder weather or snow at higher elevations, impacting trail accessibility.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by the Cueva Foradada Reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of the Moors – Cueva Foradada Reservoir loop from Alacón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate mountain bike trail that takes you past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3618839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Foradada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views of this natural feature.
tips: Nice picture that this track gives us near the town of Obón. An idyllic place for lovers of two wheels.

Montalbán is a town in the Cuencas Mineras region and is considered one of the most beautiful in it. If we are in the area we must visit the beautiful Church of Santiago or the beautiful Portal de Santa Engracia. Beautiful BTT routes leave from this town towards the Sierra de Sant Just, belonging to the Teruel Iberian System.
Near the town of Montalbán, we find these beautiful views while we ride along the Camino de Cantalobos.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Vuelta al Barranco Ancho mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult, often described as 'Red' difficulty. It requires a very good fitness level and advanced riding skills. Be prepared for strenuous sections, and you might even need to push your bike at times due to the challenging terrain and significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding, suitable for mountain bikes and gravel bikes. Given the name 'barranco' (ravine), expect undulating sections, natural paths, and potentially rocky areas or singletrack. Parts of the route coincide with the GR-262 trail, indicating a mix of established paths and more rugged sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vuelta al Barranco Ancho route?
faqs.answer: As a circular route connecting several towns, you can typically start and park in one of the towns along the itinerary, such as Montalbán, Palomar de Arroyos, or Torre de las Arcas. Check local signage for specific parking regulations in these areas.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Vuelta al Barranco Ancho trail?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit prohibitions, it's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially given the challenging nature of the trail and potential encounters with wildlife or other riders. Always practice responsible pet ownership.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride the Vuelta al Barranco Ancho are typically spring and autumn. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail or the Parque Cultural del Río Martín?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride the Vuelta al Barranco Ancho trail itself. The route passes through the Parque Cultural del Río Martín, which is an open cultural and natural park. However, always respect local regulations and any posted signs within the park regarding access or activities.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse landscapes, connecting several towns and running alongside the Martín River. You'll encounter scenic views, including a View of Obón and a View of the Camino de Cantalobos. A notable geological feature is an 'Espectacular Anticlinal' visible along the PR-TE-99 section, and you can also see the Portal de Santa Engracia.

faqs.question: Is the Vuelta al Barranco Ancho a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Vuelta al Barranco Ancho is a circular itinerary, meaning it's a loop trail. This allows you to start and finish in the same location, making logistics simpler.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route coincide with other trails. Notably, it incorporates parts of the GR-262 and also crosses sections of the Vuelta al Barranco Ancho, Sendero del Río Martín, and Camino Cantalobos.

faqs.question: What equipment should I bring for this challenging mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, it's crucial to bring appropriate gear. This includes a well-maintained mountain bike (or gravel bike), a helmet, sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit, a first-aid kit, and navigation tools. Protective gear like gloves and knee pads are also highly recommended.

faqs.question: Is the Vuelta al Barranco Ancho suitable for e-bikes or gravel bikes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is noted for its flexibility and is suitable for both e-bikes and gravel bikes, in addition to traditional mountain bikes. However, regardless of your bike type, be prepared for a challenging ride due to the significant elevation changes and varied terrain.

waypoints.name: View of the Camino de Cantalobos
